refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combines multiple obligations: terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to split these into separate FRs, each addressing a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This refinement focuses on terrain representation as one atomic FR.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a detailed and accurate representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacles or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating key site features relevant to planning and safety
Integrate terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution modules
Provide an interface for updating and validating the worksite model against operational policies and safety plans
selected_dp.selected_dp: Integrate terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution modules

potential_sources: Integration of terrain and feature data into a single model may create dependencies between data sources
coupling_check.notes: By selecting a unified worksite model DP, the design supports independence from specific data acquisition methods and allows planning/execution functions to access a consistent representation. Care must be taken to modularize data updates to avoid tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of reliable data sources for terrain and site features
Risk that changes in operational policies may require frequent updates to the model structure
Potential complexity in maintaining consistency between terrain and semantic feature data
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating terrain representation and semantic feature annotation as distinct DPs with a defined integration DP to improve modularity and reduce coupling risk.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses data integration and processing components responsible for creating a unified worksite model from terrain and feature data, providing this model to planning and execution modules.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw terrain and feature data and sends processed unified model updates to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Provides the unified worksite model to planning and execution modules for task planning and execution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as data integration and model creation logically belong here; minimal diffusion since unified model is shared but processing is centralized.
Low containment; vehicle focuses on actuation and sensing, not data integration; would cause high diffusion if forced here.
Teleoperation is for remote control, not data integration; forcing DP here would cause significant diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, unrelated to data integration; very poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for integrating terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model, as it logically centralizes data processing and model creation, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with planning and execution modules if the unified model format is not standardized.
Risk of overloading NGHE Solution with too many responsibilities if other unrelated functions are also included.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically satisfies the FR by specifying integration of terrain and feature data into a unified worksite model accessible to planning and execution modules, directly addressing the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk noted as the unified model processing is centralized within the NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion and appropriate architectural packaging for the DP. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through confirming that the NGHE Solution module produces and provides the unified worksite model to planning and execution modules as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to the NGHE Solution module for data integration and model creation, with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry and Tasking) supporting data flow and module interaction.

recommended_revisions: Standardize the unified model format to reduce interface complexity with planning and execution modules.
Monitor the scope of the NGHE Solution module to avoid overloading it with unrelated responsibilities, preserving high cohesion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and minimal diffusion risks. The NGHE Solution module is well-contained and responsibilities are clearly allocated, with feasible verification. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary complexity by not decomposing further at this stage. Recommended revisions focus on standardization and monitoring scope rather than further decomposition.

revise.why: Standardize the unified model format to reduce interface complexity and monitor the NGHE Solution module scope to maintain high cohesion and avoid overloading.

assumptions: The unified worksite model sufficiently addresses the FR for terrain and feature integration.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate architectural boundary for implementation.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity with planning and execution modules if model format is not standardized.
Risk of scope creep in the NGHE Solution module leading to reduced cohesion.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the NGHE Solution module produces the unified worksite model as specified.
Monitoring reports on module scope and interface standardization effectiveness.
